title: Landing Hero & Header Redesign
userRequest:
# Landing Hero & Header Redesign — match harvardintech.com "fresh" theme

## Goal
Take the existing uncommitted prototype and refine it to faithfully match the
live site at https://www.harvardintech.com/ (captured as reference screenshots
at Desktop 1440px and Mobile 390px). This is a site-wide visual refresh — the
brand tokens and header live in shared files, so the new look applies to every
page; the hero change is landing-specific.

## What changes

### 1. Brand tokens — `src/styles/tokens.css` (site-wide)
- Rebrand from the old blue (#048ddc) to the "fresh" theme teal:
  `--color-accent: #0ca5a5`, `--color-accent-dark: #098f8f`, and a new
  `--color-nav: #10d7d7` (brighter cyan) for nav links.
- Buttons (`.s-btn`) become pill-shaped (border-radius: 999px), sans-serif,
  16px / 700 weight, 12px×18px padding.

### 2. Header — `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro` (site-wide)
- Replace the sticky/blurred bar + left wordmark with a clean white header:
  centered nav, teal links, 15px / 500 weight, no uppercase.
- Add the Harvard shield in a white circular badge that dips from the navbar
  down into the hero image (the real site's circular logo badge).

### 3. Hero — `src/components/landing/Hero.astro` (landing)
- White uppercase title "HARVARD ALUMNI IN TECH" at 58px with the two short
  white rules flanking the text; darker image overlay (rgba(0,0,0,0.39));
  restyled body paragraph and pill Subscribe button; 200px vertical padding.
- MOBILE: scale the title down AND let it wrap to two lines (left-aligned with
  a single underline rule), matching the real site's mobile rendering rather
  than forcing one clipped line.

### 4. Asset — `public/images/harvard-shield.png`
- New shield image used in the header badge.

## Scenarios to demonstrate
- **Landing — Desktop**: the full landing page at 1440px (reuse/recapture the
  existing "Harvard in Tech - Landing Page" scenario).
- **Landing — Mobile**: the landing page at 390px, confirming the responsive
  hero title and header behave like the real site.

Production data stays empty per CodeYam's model; the landing copy is static
content, so no seed data is required for these scenarios.

## Out of scope
- No changes to the other landing sections (events, board, gallery, etc.)
  beyond what the shared token/button restyle naturally cascades.
- No CRM/admin changes.
description: Refined the uncommitted landing-page prototype to faithfully match harvardintech.com's "fresh" theme, verified against live-site reference screenshots at Desktop and Mobile. Rebranded tokens.css to the teal palette with pill-shaped buttons (site-wide), rebuilt the BaseLayout header as a centered teal nav with the Harvard shield in a white circular badge dipping into the hero (site-wide), and restyled the Hero with a white uppercase flanking-rules title over a darker overlay. Added a mobile breakpoint so the hero title scales down, wraps left-aligned with a single underline rule, and the Subscribe button goes full-width — matching the real site instead of clipping. The Harvard in Tech - Landing Page scenario now captures both Desktop and Mobile.
